title: thymus serpyllum subsp. subcitratus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. subcitratus is a subspecies of the wild thyme plant, also known as creeping thyme, a low-growing, spreading herb native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum subsp. subcitratus include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections. The health benefits of consuming Thymus serpyllum subsp. subcitratus include its ability to improve digestion, reduce stress, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. Thymus serpyllum subsp. subcitratus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used topically or inhaled to benefit from its medicinal properties.
